name: Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Vuelta al Embalse de Puentenuevo — circular desde Villaviciosa de Córdoba
description: This difficult 37.8-mile road cycling route circles the Embalse de Puentenuevo from Villaviciosa de Córdoba, offering scenic mountain views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villaviciosa De Córdoba are situated within the Sierra Morena mountains, offering a diverse and challenging terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by rugged landscapes, extensive pine and oak forests, and significant elevation changes, providing a demanding experience. Rivers like the Guadiato and Névalo traverse the area, alongside features such as the Embalse de Puente Nuevo reservoir, which contribute to the varied scenery. This mountainous environment ensures routes often include climbs and descents, appealing to experienced road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Villaviciosa De Córdoba The most popular road cycling route is Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cerro Muriano summit – Los Villares Periurban Park loop from Cerro Muriano, a moderate 24.1 miles (38.7 km) path. This route includes a climb to Cerro Muriano summit and passes through the periurban park. Local road cyclists also love the Villaviciosa de Córdoba – Los Arenales Bridge loop from Villaviciosa de Córdoba, a 31.1 miles (50.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Villaviciosa De Córdoba is defined by rugged Sierra Morena landscapes, dense pine forests, and challenging climbs, with routes often featuring reservoirs and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Villaviciosa De Córdoba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.6 stars from around 10 reviews. More than 450 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villaviciosa De Córdoba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes documented around Villaviciosa De Córdoba, offering a variety of experiences through the Sierra Morena mountains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: The terrain is characterized by the rugged Sierra Morena mountains, featuring significant elevation changes with challenging climbs and descents. You'll cycle through extensive pine and oak forests, past rivers like the Guadiato and Névalo, and alongside reservoirs such as the Embalse de Puente Nuevo.

Q: Are there road cycling routes for different skill levels in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: Yes, the routes cater to various abilities. While the area is known for its demanding terrain, there is 1 easy route, 11 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes available, ensuring options for most road cyclists.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: You can encounter several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/624215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Popea Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are a notable highlight, and you might also pass by various springs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4700544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;First Spring of the Bejarano Stream&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcubilla Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The Embalse de Puente Nuevo reservoir is also a prominent feature in the landscape.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers historical points of interest. You might come across ancient mills like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4951467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino del Molinillo&lt;/a&gt;, or even the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/632043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medina Azahara Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, which is a short distance from some routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4646005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of the Seven Corners&lt;/a&gt; also adds a historical touch to the landscape.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with stargazing in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: Absolutely. Villaviciosa de Córdoba is part of the Sierra Morena Starlight Reserve, one of the world's largest. This designation means minimal light pollution, making it an excellent location for stargazing after your daytime rides. An Observatorio Starlight is available for visitors.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010631721/puente-nuevo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 60.8 km route with over 940 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding experience around the reservoir.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes suitable for a good workout?
A: Yes, there are 11 moderate routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38283926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Muriano summit – Los Villares Periurban Park loop from Cerro Muriano&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, covering 38.7 km and including a climb to Cerro Muriano summit.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Villaviciosa De Córdoba are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviciosa de Córdoba – Los Arenales Bridge loop from Villaviciosa de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt; is a 50.1 km circular trail that takes you through varied terrain.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.6 stars from around 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the rugged Sierra Morena landscapes, dense pine forests, and the challenging climbs that define the region's routes.

Q: Is Villaviciosa De Córdoba suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: While the region is known for its challenging, mountainous terrain, there is 1 easy route available. For families, it's advisable to check the specific route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches the family's fitness and experience levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: Andalusia, where Villaviciosa de Córdoba is located, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer months. Spring and autumn typically provide milder temperatures, making them ideal for tackling the region's climbs and enjoying the scenic routes.
tips: From this bridge we can see the Guadiato River that feeds the Puente Nuevo Reservoir.

This reservoir is located in the municipality of Espiel. Built in the years 1956-1973. Its function apart from water supply is also to generate electricity, it is a place to do water sports, bathing or fishing.
It is located in the Valle del Guadiato region, at an altitude of 693 meters and 43 kilometers from the capital.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The Tour around the Embalse de Puentenuevo is a challenging ride that typically takes around 3 hours to complete. This estimate is for the full 60.8-kilometer circular route.

faqs.question: How difficult is the cycling route around Embalse de Puentenuevo?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult road ride, requiring very good fitness due to its demanding terrain and a significant elevation gain of 940 meters. It's not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a combination of demanding terrain and scenic views. While primarily a road cycling route, some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride. The area immediately surrounding the Puente Nuevo Reservoir is relatively flat, but the overall route also includes mountainous sections.

faqs.question: Where does the cycling tour start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular tour starts from Villaviciosa de Córdoba. While specific parking details aren't provided, as a settlement, Villaviciosa de Córdoba typically offers parking options for visitors.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the picturesque Puente Nuevo Reservoir, offering tranquil views surrounded by pine trees and mountains. You will also cross the Bridge over the Guadiato River.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle around Embalse de Puentenuevo?
faqs.answer: The trail research does not specify a best time of year. However, given its location in Córdoba, Spain, spring and autumn generally offer more moderate temperatures for cycling compared to the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a difficult road ride requiring 'very good fitness.' It is not suitable for beginners or families looking for an easy cycling experience.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The trail research does not provide specific information regarding dogs on this cycling route. It's generally advisable to check local regulations or signage regarding pets in natural areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this tour?
faqs.answer: There is no information in the provided data indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route around the Embalse de Puentenuevo.

faqs.question: Are there any places to stop for refreshments or food along the way?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Villaviciosa de Córdoba, where you can find amenities. The trail itself is described as a 'quiet place,' suggesting limited facilities directly along the reservoir loop. It's best to carry your own provisions.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino Mozárabe and the Sendero de Sierra Morena.
